Despite his severe injuries, Hazeltine remained alert during transport to Interim LSU Hospital and even joked about his ordeal in the ambulance, though he faces concerns about potential vision loss in one eye.

Lord Janner, an 86-year-old former Labour peer facing child sex allegations, will escape prosecution due to Alzheimer's disease, but abuse campaigners have angrily questioned this decision after he signed a letter on April 9 expressing his wish to remain in the House of Lords—suggesting he was competent enough to face court. The controversy has intensified with revelations of a potential conflict of interest involving the Director of Public Prosecutions' adviser and Lord Janner's QC son Daniel, raising concerns about whether the decision not to charge Janner was made fairly. Campaigners and figures like the rabbi and daughter of the peer argue the situation is hypocritical, contending that if Janner is well enough to serve as a legislator and sign official documents, he should be fit enough to stand trial for the child sex allegations against him.

Turkey has blocked access to Twitter and YouTube after the platforms refused to remove pictures of prosecutor Mehmet Selim Kiraz during the deadly armed siege in which he was taken hostage and killed. The 46-year-old prosecutor died in the hospital after members of the far-left Revolutionary People's Liberation Party-Front stormed a courthouse last week, leaving his family to grieve over images of the violence that Turkish authorities claimed deeply upset them. This represents another instance of Turkey imposing restrictions on social media platforms, following previous blocks related to political content.

# Summary

David and Victoria Beckham have won a planning battle against their next-door neighbour over proposals to install air-conditioning in five rooms of their £31.5 million west London mansion, a Grade II listed building. While one neighbour opposed the plans citing concerns about noise, vibration and impact on the historic character of the area, the Royal Borough of Kensington and Chelsea Council approved the proposals, determining that the air-conditioning would have minimal impact on the listed building. Estate agents have estimated the property could be worth as much as £42 million when the Beckhams complete their restoration of the mansion, which they purchased in cash in 2013.

California Governor Jerry Brown ordered a 25 percent overall cutback in water use by cities and towns as the state faces a fourth summer of drought with state reservoirs holding only a year's worth of water and groundwater levels falling due to record low snowfall, already taking its toll on the Technicolor landscape of lush yards, emerald golf courses, and aquamarine swimming pools. The mandatory cuts target everyone from homeowners to industrial facilities, requiring them to replace irrigated lawns with drought-resistant vegetation and prohibiting new homes from using drinking water for irrigation without water-stingy drip systems, with violators subject to fines. While homeowners will receive rebates for lawn replacement and water-efficient appliances, critics note the exemption of agriculture—which consumes 80 percent of California's water—may undermine public support, as affluent Southern California communities currently use over 300 gallons of water per person daily compared to 70 gallons in the Bay Area.

Universal's "Furious 7," the final film featuring the late Paul Walker, is expected to gross $115 million or more in its North American opening this weekend, shattering records as the top April title ever and the widest release in Universal's history with approximately 4,003 theaters. The film, which resumed production after Walker's death in a car crash in November 2013 through the use of CGI and his brothers as stand-ins, is poised to achieve the largest three-day opening since "The Hunger Games: Catching Fire" in 2013, surpassing even "Furious 6"'s franchise-record $97.4 million three-day debut, with a global debut exceeding $250 million across 10,500 screens in 63 territories.

Chelsea midfielder Cesc Fabregas has seen his form significantly decline in the second half of the season, continuing his struggle when the Spanish midfielder captained Spain to a 2-0 defeat against Holland on Tuesday. After accumulating 15 Premier League assists by mid-January, Fabregas has managed only one assist in his last five games, putting him on track to fall short of Thierry Henry's Barclays Premier League record of 20 assists, though an assist for Diego Costa in Chelsea's last game suggests a potential late-season resurgence. With nine games remaining, Jose Mourinho will be hoping his midfielder can rediscover his early-season form to help the club secure the Premier League title.

A lawsuit with 750 plaintiffs is seeking $1 billion from Johns Hopkins University for its role in approving and helping to plan a horrific 1945-1956 medical experiment in Guatemala where the U.S. deliberately infected approximately 700 individuals—including orphans, soldiers, prisoners, and prostitutes—with syphilis to test drugs and study the disease. The victims and their families, including Marta Cesarea Pérez Ruiz whose children were born severely handicapped after her husband passed on the infection, claim those infected suffered devastating consequences including death from syphilis and transmission of the disease to their offspring, resulting in babies born blind or without brains. The U.S. government only admitted to the experiment in 2010, and it was conducted by Dr. John Cutler, who also oversaw the infamous Tuskegee syphilis experiment in Alabama.

# Summary

Hip-hop star Nelly was arrested in Tennessee on drug charges after a state trooper pulled over his private bus on Interstate 40 and detected marijuana odor emanating from the vehicle. The Tennessee Department of Safety and Homeland Security stated that troopers found methamphetamine, marijuana, drug paraphernalia, handguns, and small Ziploc bags commonly used for selling drugs during their search. The 40-year-old rapper from St. Louis was charged with felony possession of drugs, simple possession of marijuana, and possession of drug paraphernalia before posting bond and being released from Putnam County Jail.

# Summary

A Japanese woman exacted revenge on her cheating boyfriend by submerging his entire Apple collection—including an iMac, iPhone, iPad, and accessories—in a bathtub, then photographing the "gadget Jacuzzi" and sending the images to him. The water damage to his prized electronics proved irreversible, and when the unnamed man posted the photos on Twitter, the post went viral with over 15,400 retweets, generating mixed reactions from English-speaking users. While some Apple fans criticized the woman's harsh actions, many others supported her revenge as justified payback for his infidelity.

Bayern Munich defeated Borussia Dortmund 1-0 in a Bundesliga clash, with Robert Lewandowski heading against his former club to condemn the hosts to defeat at the Westfalenstadion. Charismatic Dortmund manager Jurgen Klopp conceded Bayern deserved victory, noting his side lacked punch against their deep, physically robust setup and failed to generate enough chances, though Lewandowski's goal sent the visitors' bench into raptures as Pep Guardiola's side moved 10 points clear at the top. However, Dortmund captain Mats Hummels disagreed, arguing his team created sufficient opportunities to merit a better result.

President Barack Obama arrived in Jamaica late Wednesday and visited the island nation on Thursday, becoming the first American president to visit since Ronald Reagan in 1982, where Jamaican Prime Minister Portia Simpson Miller expressed her love and admiration for him while requesting he exonerate Marcus Garvey. Obama then traveled to Panama City for the Summit of the Americas, where Secretary of State John Kerry met with Cuban Foreign Minister Bruno Rodriguez for constructive talks, and Obama signaled he will soon remove Cuba from the U.S. list of state sponsors of terrorism to improve relations with Cuban President Raul Castro. The historic summit marks the first time Cuba is included, presenting an opportunity to reshape U.S.-Cuba relations after decades of estrangement, with plans for Obama to visit the Panama Canal and other regional sites.

# Summary

During his Easter Mass address, Pope Francis reminded the world of the Vatican's moral authority while lamenting the suffering caused by ongoing conflicts in Iraq, Syria, the Holy Land, Libya, Ukraine, Yemen, Nigeria, and other regions, calling for an end to bloodshed and humanitarian aid for those in need. He emphasized the immense humanitarian tragedy unfolding in these areas and urged the international community to support the peace process between Israelis and Palestinians while also addressing economic oppression, drug trafficking, and corruption. The Pope concluded his "urbi et orbi" address by consoling the marginalized, poor, sick, and suffering around the world.

Irina Shayk, the 29-year-old Russian supermodel, stars in Linda Farrow eyewear's steamy Spring Summer 2015 campaign titled "Wanderlust," filmed writhing around an LA pool with hunky male model Jarrod Scott. The behind-the-scenes video shows Irina emerging from glistening water in plunging metallic swimsuits while sporting the retro-inspired sunglasses collection, including eye-catching gold-plated frames, with moments of Jarrod nuzzling into Irina's neck as they pose for photographer Mariano Vivanco. The campaign showcases Irina looking incredible in various oversized frames and metallic styles, with more affordable alternatives available from brands like Karen Walker for those seeking the retro round-frame sunglasses look.

A Polish prince named Janek Zylinski, whose father Captain Andrzej Zylinski was a war hero who fought the Nazis in 1939, has challenged Ukip leader Nigel Farage to a sword duel in Hyde Park, posting the challenge online in protest of discrimination against Polish people and migrants. Mr Zylinski said he had "had enough" of Farage's immigration stance and offered an alternative "duel of words" if preferred, declaring that such a resolution would be fitting for a self-respecting 18th-century Polish aristocrat and English gentleman. The challenge came as Farage was campaigning and defending Ukip's immigration policies during a factory visit in Essex.

Calbuco volcano in southern Chile erupted twice without warning, with the first eruption at 6pm local time sending ash plumes over six miles into the atmosphere and forcing the evacuation of 4,000 people from nearby towns including Puerto Varas and Puerto Montt, where residents described panic as ash blanketed the La Ensenada locality and areas up to 18 miles away. The volcano, one of Chile's 90 active volcanoes last active in 1961, ranks as a three or four on the Volcanic Explosivity Index, potentially releasing ten billion cubic meters of ash that could spread across the Pacific to Australia. Volcanoes like Calbuco occur at the edges of tectonic plates where magma from the Earth's core forces through weak points in the crust, causing eruptions that have damaged infrastructure, prompted flight cancellations, and raised serious health concerns for residents throughout the region.

Dani Alves' contract with Barcelona expired on June 30, sparking a contract wrangle after the club offered him a reduced two-year deal with performance clauses, while Alves' camp disputes the terms and claims one big club (likely Manchester United at Old Trafford) has offered him a three-year deal without such restrictions. Despite posting cryptic social media messages and receiving interest from multiple clubs, Alves remains hopeful Barcelona will improve their offer so he can stay in Spain, where his children live and where he has spent 13 successful years—though Barcelona's FIFA transfer ban makes his departure increasingly likely. The 32-year-old remains one of the most gifted right-backs of his generation; his loss would leave Barcelona dependent on Douglas at the position, and his defensive tenacity and recovery speed would serve well under a manager less inclined to have him flying forward in the Barcelona style.

Kate Temple, a 33-year-old hairdresser, was jailed for 20 months after admitting she ripped another woman's ear off with her teeth during a scuffle at a charity hog roast event in County Durham. The attack on victim Shelley Williams occurred after Miss Williams accused Temple of being a "homewrecker" and having an affair with her partner, leaving the victim covered in blood with a large portion of her ear bitten away. Temple attempted to buy another drink from the bar before police became involved following the incident.

Jenson Button believes McLaren have made significant progress with their new Honda power unit since the start of the new Formula One season and now have their rivals in their sights ahead of this weekend's Chinese Grand Prix in Shanghai. Although both Button and team-mate Fernando Alonso retired during the Malaysian Grand Prix, the British driver was encouraged to see their car was competitive and able to race other midfield runners like Force India and Sauber. Button acknowledged the team still has work to do on downforce and the power unit, but expressed optimism that McLaren is moving in the right direction and hopes to continue picking off competitors race by race.

Claudia Martins, a 33-year-old Portuguese national and mother-of-five, has been convicted of manslaughter by reason of diminished responsibility after killing her newborn baby daughter by suffocating her with toilet paper at her sister's flat in Bristol. Martins kept her pregnancy secret and gave birth alone, then wrapped the newborn baby inside a suitcase lined with towels and a plastic bag, where it remained undiscovered for three days until her sister noticed a smell. Although she was acquitted of murder, a post-mortem examination confirmed that toilet paper had been deliberately inserted into the baby's mouth.

# Summary

AP McCoy will ride Paul Nicholls-trained Mr Mole in the Grade One Celebration Chase at Sandown on Saturday, marking his last ride in a Grade One race before retiring as a professional jockey. Mr Mole is installed as one of three 7-2 co-favourites alongside Irish raider Special Tiara and Nicky Henderson's Sprinter Sacre. McCoy shocked the racing world by announcing his intention to quit riding after previously winning on Mr Mole at Newbury in February.

# Summary

A woman in her 20s who could not swim was rescued from the River Cam in Cambridge after frantically struggling to stay afloat when she tumbled from a punt that had collided with another boat. She disappeared under the water several times while screaming for help before her friends and punt hire staff pulled her to safety. The dramatic rescue occurred on a day when Britain experienced unseasonably warm Easter weather, with temperatures reaching nearly 21°C—particularly in Northumberland—prompting families to flock to beaches and parks across the UK to enjoy the balmy conditions.

The article praises Marlon Samuels' disciplined century in the second Test against England—earned through a demanding duel with Ben Stokes—and Jason Holder's efforts in Antigua, highlighting how their character and application exemplify the winning culture that new West Indies coach Phil Simmons is building. The author argues that Test cricket, despite being slower than limited-overs cricket, offers greater mental and physical challenge, pointing to examples like Jonathan Trott's psychological battle after an 18-month absence or Alastair Cook overcoming technical issues discovered by opponents, and contends that West Indies must focus on completing five-day Tests before retaining their best players. While acknowledging Twenty20's appeal, the author emphasizes that Test cricket—where bowlers spend entire series like England's 130-over effort in Karachi grinding for victory—is ultimately what players will be remembered for, as it demands exceptional perseverance that no other form can replicate.

Officer Michael Slager fatally shot Walter Scott multiple times in the back during a traffic stop, leaving the handcuffed man dead with five gunshot wounds, but police reports omitted these critical details and falsely claimed officers administered CPR when video evidence shows they did not. The killer cop would have gotten away with the cover-up had a bystander not recorded video showing Slager picking up his Taser and placing it near Scott's body, while his seven responding fellow officers corroborated the fabricated account in their incident reports. This case highlights a potential pattern of police misconduct in North Charleston, as the video evidence directly contradicted the official narrative that would have allowed Slager to escape accountability.

Nina Moric, a model who appeared in Ricky Martin's music video for "La Vida Loco," denied Italian media claims that she attempted to take her own life, instead posting on her Instagram account that she collapsed due to low blood pressure and was hospitalized on Saturday night with heavy bruising. Ms. Moric explained that she fell and hit her head during a domestic accident while her boyfriend Luigi Mario Favoloso was in Naples, reassuring her 40,000 Instagram followers that she was recovering well at home. The Croatian-born model, who Italian media claimed had previously attempted suicide in 2009, expressed shock at the reports and stated she only needed rest to recover from her fall.

Juventus advanced to their first Champions League semi-final since 2002-03 after Massimiliano Allegri's side's defeat of Monaco came via a 0-0 draw at the Stade Louis II on Wednesday night, progressing 1-0 on aggregate. Patrice Evra, who prevented Monaco's Yannick Ferreira Carrasco from delivering several crosses during the match, believes the Italians employed quintessential defensive traits and sat back strategically, describing their approach as "solid" but "ugly"—yet suggested that Ligue 1 elite clubs could learn from adopting a more aggressive, combative style of play. Evra also urged compatriot Paul Pogba not to rush his return from a hamstring injury and to have faith in the medical staff.

At Omaha's Henry Doorley Zoo in Nebraska yesterday, a little girl was playfully beating her chest at a gorilla through a pane of glass when a second silverback—apparently less amused by the game—charged at the window with such force that the glass cracked upon impact, sending the terrified family fleeing to safety. The incident, captured on video, garnered over 126,000 YouTube views and sparked discussion about the power of male mountain gorillas, which can weigh up to 485 pounds. Henry Doorley Zoo is renowned for its gorilla breeding program and maintains the world's largest gorilla sperm bank.

John Suchet, the Classic FM presenter, is mourning the death of his wife Bonnie, who passed away peacefully on April 15, 2015, at age 73 following a decade-long battle with dementia that began when Suchet first noticed behavioral changes in March 2004. The 71-year-old broadcaster has been openly vocal about the devastating toll his wife's diagnosis took on their relationship, writing a book and becoming honorary president of Dementia UK to help others facing similar struggles. In recent years, Suchet has become a prominent dementia advocate, describing how their once grand passion—which began when Bonnie flew to meet him during his time as a US correspondent—ultimately required her placement in residential care, a transition that led to his career move to Classic FM as a way to rebuild his life.

Ben and Shelby Offrink, who met in college in Caledonia, Michigan, are both fighting rare and aggressive cancers while raising two young daughters—Shelby battling an intramedullary glioblastoma that has spread to her brain with three inoperable tumors, while Ben fights a recurrence of Hodgkins lymphoma and hopes to eventually undergo a bone marrow transplant. Despite devastating diagnoses with prognoses of six months for Shelby and a year for Ben, they remain determined to fight, with Shelby nicknaming her tumors the "Tumah Tumah-nator" and both seeking advanced clinical trials at centers like Harvard and Mayo Clinic, according to Ben and supported by her older brother Jay Tomczak's encouragement. Bolstered by an extraordinary global community that has sent care packages from Ireland, Sweden, and Australia, the couple refuses to give up, drawing strength from family and their resolve to survive for their daughters.

Real Madrid boss Carlo Ancelotti faces an enviable but complex selection puzzle, with Spanish papers reporting seven players vying for six spots in his preferred formation, though attacking prowess remains undeniable following the weekend's nine-goal demolition of Granada. PSG's thrilling 3-2 win at title rivals Marseille has propelled them to the top of Ligue 1 with 62 points, one ahead of Lyon, demonstrating the league's growing competitive appeal. Elsewhere, fallen giants Inter Milan grapple with internal divisions between management over the club's direction.

Floyd Mayweather took time out of his training session at the Mayweather Boxing Club to pose for a picture with Motown legend Gladys Knight, just four days before he goes head-to-head with rival Manny Pacquiao in the "Fight of the Century" on May 2 at the MGM Grand in Las Vegas. Knight tweeted about meeting "The Champ" Mayweather and expressed her support, with Mayweather thanking her for coming to watch him train ahead of Saturday's fight. This blockbuster bout is expected to be the biggest event in boxing history, with Mayweather set to earn £120 million.

Ed Miliband, the Labour leader who has struggled in personal popularity polls against David Cameron, has hired the leadership coaching firm ExtendedMind to help him improve his communication skills and project authenticity—a stated focus on "The Politics of Authenticity"—despite already employing Barack Obama's former adviser David Axelrod for £300,000. The firm, which uses neuroscience and business psychology techniques to develop what it calls "intelligent networks" for coalition-building, claims it can help politicians improve their body language, breathing, and overall presence—coaching that Miliband appears to need after his infamous reliance on a lectern during campaign events, including one in a Leicestershire factory car park where he even launched Labour campaign billboards. The hiring has drawn criticism from both Labour MPs questioning the value of Axelrod's involvement and Conservative opponents who argue that no amount of coaching can give Miliband the authenticity needed to lead the country on the world stage.

# Summary

Raymond Townsend, a manager at US Limousine on Long Island, New York, was found liable for sexual harassment against his dispatcher employee Geralyn Ganci after subjecting her to more than a year of explicit messages and unwanted sexual advances, ultimately firing her when she refused his sexual requests. The case, which included evidence of text messages where Townsend explicitly stated he fired Ganci for refusing to have sex with him, resulted in a judgment requiring Townsend and US Limousine to pay over $700,000 in damages and legal fees. Ganci had originally sought $5 million in damages for the emotional distress caused by the harassment.

Bayern Munich playmaker Thiago made his emotional return to football on Saturday, coming on for Philipp Lahm in Bayern's 1-0 victory against Borussia Dortmund at the Westfalenstadion, his first appearance in over a year since suffering a serious knee injury in March 2014. The Spanish midfielder expressed his gratitude to the club, coaches, and team-mates for their support, moved by the fans' reaction and reminded of how much football means to him. Though not yet fit for full 90-minute appearances, Thiago remains determined to win games and titles with Bayern Munich.

David Cameron today accused the BBC of spreading falsehoods after presenter Andrew Marr claimed on Sunday's Andrew Marr Show that the PM's favourite sport was foxhunting, which Cameron denied. Turning the clock back to ape John Major's election soapbox strategy, Cameron climbed onto a wooden pallet to address party activists, defending the Tory record and pledging to "take to the streets" to make his case to voters. Although BBC presenter Andrew Marr later admitted his mistake was a "cock up not conspiracy," the incident highlighted tensions between the government and the media during the campaign.

Gwyneth Paltrow is participating in the #FoodBankNYCChallenge by showcasing a week's worth of groceries for approximately $29—the weekly amount allocated under Agriculture's Thrifty Food Plan for people living on food stamps. Celebrity chef Mario Batali nominated Paltrow for the challenge, which asks participants to subsist on just $1.38 per meal for seven days to understand the struggles families face affording food, according to the Food Bank for New York City's website. The challenge was launched in response to recent Congressional cuts to food stamps since 2013, which have led to increased visitors at soup kitchens and food pantries across the city.

Manchester United's Old Trafford megastore staff, including the club's official mascot Fred the Red, face uncertain futures as Nike's agreement with the club expires on July 31, following Manchester United's world-record £750m kit deal with adidas. Though Nike claims to be in constant communication with the club and conducting consultations with employees, staff members express worry about their job security, with some already leaving due to uncertainty. Fred the Red, who has represented the club since 1994, is among dozens of workers—many earning minimum wage—concerned about whether they will be re-employed when the megastore transitions to club management.

Liverpool edged past Blackburn Rovers in their FA Cup sixth-round replay, with Philippe Coutinho eventually breaking the deadlock after 161 goalless minutes to send the Reds to Wembley, while goalkeeper Simon Eastwood came agonizingly close to equalizing in stoppage time. Blackburn's defense, anchored by a back line that kept Liverpool's main threats of Raheem Sterling and Daniel Sturridge quieter than expected through resolute defending, couldn't quite stop Coutinho's winner despite a commanding performance that tested Liverpool throughout. Liverpool's midfield ultimately proved decisive in overcoming Blackburn's well-organized challenge, with Coutinho's clinical finish deciding a closely contested encounter at Ewood Park.

A heart-warming video created by Pandora to celebrate Mother's Day 2015 features six blindfolded children who attempt to recognize their mothers without seeing them, relying instead on touch and scent. As the children move tentatively along the row, taking the women's hands and touching their hair and faces, each child successfully locates their own mother, resulting in emotional reunions. The video, which has been viewed 2.8 million times, demonstrates the special bond between mother and child and resonated so strongly with audiences that celebrities like Ashton Kutcher shared it on social media.

Salford assistant coach Ian Watson, 38, is poised to come out of playing retirement to answer an SOS for Sunday's home game against Castleford after the Red Devils lost four players to suspension and three more to injury. If Watson plays, he would become the oldest player currently in Super League, surpassing Adrian Morley. Salford has already signed veteran hooker Wayne Godwin on loan and borrowed reinforcements from Wigan to field a competitive squad.

21-year-old Jordan Spieth delivered a stunning opening round of 64 at the 79th Masters, sinking a 25-foot birdie putt on the 18th to become the youngest first-round leader in Masters history with a three-shot advantage. Justin Rose made the early running with an impressive 67, tying Charley Hoffman and Jason Day for second place. Rory McIlroy recovered from a tentative front nine to shoot 71, demonstrating poise after struggling with the course's par fives in his opening stretch.

England captain Alastair Cook must somehow shut out the chaos surrounding English cricket to focus on a crucial three-Test series against West Indies beginning Monday, which represents one of the most important Test series of his captaincy. Despite the institutional turmoil—from Paul Downton's sacking to speculation about Kevin Pietersen's future—the imminent first Test has barely registered, yet England really should defeat this inexperienced West Indies team, though slow pitches and a determined opponent could make it a harder battle than expected. Cook is determined to concentrate solely on the series ahead while the broader changes enveloping English cricket swirl around him.

The Pentagon announced Tuesday plans to exhume and identify the remains of nearly 400 sailors and Marines killed when the USS Oklahoma was hit by nine torpedoes and capsized during Japan's December 7, 1941 surprise attack on Pearl Harbor. Of the 429 sailors and Marines onboard, only 35 were identified immediately after, with the remaining hundreds buried as unknowns at Honolulu's National Memorial Cemetery of the Pacific since 1950. Advances in forensic science and genealogical DNA samples from family members now make it possible to identify up to 388 servicemen within five years, offering long-awaited closure to families.

YouTube entertainer Yousef Saleh Erakat disguised himself as a homeless man in a new video, offering money to strangers on the street to explore how people react when someone perceived as "lesser" extends generosity to them. Most reacted negatively—one man threw the $10 bill back, another told him to "get out of here," and a third became hostile when offered assistance—while only two people graciously accepted or reciprocated. Through the experiment, Erakat revealed that true wealth comes from humility and a loving heart rather than material possessions, arguing that it doesn't matter how much money you have if it takes away from being humble.

Eighteen-year-old Longwood University student Anjelica "AJ" Hadsell has been missing since March 2, and her grandmother Katherine Hadsell criticizes Norfolk police for not doing enough to find the girl who disappeared while home for spring break. Police recently focused their search on a pond near Carrsville after receiving a tip that her cell phone may be submerged there, while her stepfather Wesley Hadsell was arrested and charged with breaking and entering after he illegally searched a Norfolk area home looking for his stepdaughter. The case remains unsolved with police not ruling out foul play and having no official suspects.

Australian researchers from the Royal Melbourne Institute of Technology have developed a drone called Joggobot, a new jogging buddy designed to fly alongside runners and keep them motivated by maintaining a consistent pace—technology that could lock onto a pattern on a runner's shirt to track their speed and direction. In their study, the researchers explained how 13 joggers ran with a quadcopter companion for 15-40 minutes and reported that the drone acted as an effective "pace keeper," though some expressed safety concerns about the device deviating from its path. The team claims that robotic companions could support various physical activities such as cycling, cross-country skiing, and rowing, with potential features like creating a draft for cyclists or displaying encouraging messages to boost motivation.

# Summary

Matthew Bird from New Zealand quit his $70,000-a-year job as an agricultural adviser to become a mechanic earning a meagre $38,000 annually in order to avoid a $600-a-month hike in child support payments. After Inland Revenue amended their child support formula in April, his monthly payments increased from $1,300 to $1,900, but by reducing his income, he managed to lower his payments to $554 a month. The article highlights how the new child support formula has prompted some parents to consider changing jobs to reduce their payments, with Matthew Bird stating he is "on half wage but pay a third of what I used to."

British man Navinder Singh Sarao, 36, was accused this week of causing the 2010 Wall Street 'Flash Crash' and making £26 million from illegal trades over five years, with US authorities suspecting he was making illicit trades back in 2009 despite being warned by the Chicago Mercantile Exchange. Although the CME warned Sarao about his trading in 2009, he allegedly continued his market manipulation strategies eight more times over six years, triggering the Flash Crash on May 6, 2010, which wiped £570 billion from US stock values in just five minutes. Sarao faces both civil charges from the CFTC and criminal charges from the Department of Justice, with experts bewildered at why authorities took six years to act despite early suspicions of his fraudulent trading activity.

Canadian expat Lisa Webb, who moved to France in 2009, initially feared her vegetable-phobic daughter would starve when attending French preschool, but documented her discovery on her blog Canadian Expat Mom that children were served sophisticated three-course meals like roast beef and turkey cordon bleu and ate everything put in front of them without coming home hungry. After observing lunch in her daughter's school canteen, Lisa was impressed by how French children used real cutlery and plates, sat politely at tables, and consumed healthy meals including vegetables—a stark contrast to the quick, casual lunch periods she remembered from Canadian schools. Lisa attributes this success to France's cultural respect for gastronomy and dining etiquette, where children learn to appreciate fine food from age three, resulting in adventurous eaters who enjoy restaurant dining without needing children's menus.

# Summary

According to a survey of 1,000 people conducted by personal budgeting software company YouNeedaBudget.com, shopping with friends encourages people to spend far more money than they would alone, with 64 per cent of adults confessing they overspend when shopping with friends due to showing off and peer pressure. Despite 85 per cent of participants setting budgets before shopping, 95 per cent admitted they spend more than planned, with top overspending categories including food (58 per cent) and clothing (54 per cent). The survey also found that nearly two-thirds of Americans would actually prefer to shop alone rather than with friends to avoid excessive spending and buyer's remorse.

Ellie Grant, 22, appeared on ITV's This Morning to discuss how she suddenly developed Tourette's syndrome at age 21 and found love with her deaf fiancé Jordan, who switches off his hearing aid during her tics. During the interview, Miss Grant suffered a verbal tic and blurted out "silver fox" at presenter Phillip Schofield, leaving him bemused and speechless as co-presenter Amanda Holden kindly chimed in with support. Miss Grant, who initially worried she'd never find a boyfriend who could tolerate her inappropriate outbursts that began with shouting "marshmallow" in a supermarket, found her perfect match in Jordan—a pairing her mother aptly dubbed the "Undateables."

Adam Gadahn, 36, a U.S. citizen known as al Qaeda's American propagandist attacking his native country, was killed in January in a U.S. counterterrorism operation, the White House announced Thursday. Gadahn, who was born in California and converted to Islam before traveling through London to Pakistan in 1998 to join al Qaeda, rose through the terrorist organization's ranks to become its chief English-language propagandist, appearing in numerous videos threatening attacks on American and Western interests. Though not among al Qaeda's top leaders, he was one of its most prominent members and was indicted for treason and material support to terrorism in 2006, with his death also resulting in the deaths of U.S. hostage Warren Weinstein and another al Qaeda operative Ahmed Farouq.

# Summary

Formula One driver Jenson Button, 35, completed the London Marathon 2015 in an impressive time of 2 hours, 52 minutes, and 30 seconds, finishing just behind former Olympic rower James Cracknell. Running on behalf of Cancer Research charity, Button praised the amazing atmosphere and collective spirit of the 36,000 runners, describing the experience as "really really emotional" and highlighting the effort put in by all participants. The McLaren driver told the BBC he was "chuffed to bits" with his performance and moved by the goodwill and unity demonstrated throughout the event.
